private.name: ClearSpace

data.narrative:
Vyoma is a Series A space and aerospace company founded by Stefan Frey and Luisa Buinhas, having raised €15M+ to date. Valuation analysis draws on a peer set of five same-sector, same/adjacent-stage private companies: Aalyria ($1.3B, Series B), Stoke Space ($1.0B, Series B), ClearSpace ($400M, Series A), Hubble Network ($400M, Series B), and Sift ($274M, Series B).

The peer-median method is the applicable triangulation approach here. The five comparable companies span a range from $274M to $1.3B, with a median valuation of $400M. This median reflects both Series A and Series B stage companies operating within space infrastructure and related subsectors. The public-comp anchoring method, while available (six public Space & Aerospace tickers trade at a median 45.3x P/S), cannot be applied due to absence of Vyoma's revenue data, typical for private companies at this stage.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $340M to $460M, centered on $400M. This range is narrow and rests on a single quantitative method, reflecting limited data points rather than triangulation across multiple independent approaches. Key uncertainties include Vyoma's revenue trajectory relative to peers, the timing and scale of near-term customer contracts, and the degree to which Series B comparables (Stoke, Aalyria) represent appropriate anchors for a Series A company. Stage-adjusted discount to the peer median may be warranted pending disclosure of operational metrics.
